heading: Administration of the National Historical Park

children.heading: General administration
children.label: 1 General administration
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior (in this subchapter referred to as the “Secretary”) shall administer the park in accordance with this subchapter and the provisions of law generally applicable to units of the National Park System, including the Act entitled “An Act to establish a National Park Service, and for other purposes”, approved (; through 4), and the Act of (; ).August 25, 1916August 21, 193539 Stat. 53516 U.S.C. 1149 Stat. 66616 U.S.C. 461–4671See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Specific authorities
children.label: 2 Specific authorities
children.text: The Secretary may interpret the story of Rosie the Riveter and the World War II home front, conduct and maintain oral histories that relate to the World War II home front theme, and provide technical assistance in the preservation of historic properties that support this story.

children.heading: Nystrom Elementary School
children.label: 3 Nystrom Elementary School
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter affects the authority of the West Contra Costa Unified School District to administer Nystrom Elementary School.

children.heading: General agreements
children.label: 1 General agreements
children.text: The Secretary may enter into cooperative agreements with the owners of the Child Development Field Centers (Ruth C. Powers) (Maritime), Atchison Housing, the Kaiser-Permanente Field Hospital, and Richmond Fire Station 67A, pursuant to which the Secretary may mark, interpret, improve, restore, and provide technical assistance with respect to the preservation and interpretation of such properties. Such agreements shall contain, but need not be limited to, provisions under which the Secretary shall have the right of access at reasonable times to public portions of the property for interpretive and other purposes, and that no changes or alterations shall be made in the property except by mutual agreement.

children.label: A
children.text: the Ford Assembly Building;

children.label: B
children.text: the intact dry docks/basin docks and five historic structures at Richmond Shipyard #3;

children.label: C
children.text: the Shimada Peace Memorial Park;

children.label: D
children.text: Westshore Park;

children.label: E
children.text: the Rosie the Riveter Memorial;

children.label: F
children.text: Sheridan Observation Point Park;

children.label: G
children.text: the Bay Trail/Esplanade;

children.label: H
children.text: Vincent Park; and

children.label: I
children.text: the vessel S.S. RED OAK VICTORY, and Whirley Cranes associated with shipbuilding in Richmond.

children.heading: West Contra Costa Unified School District
children.label: 3 West Contra Costa Unified School District

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may enter into cooperative agreements with the West Contra Costa Unified School District and other appropriate public and private agencies, organizations, and institutions to carry out the purposes of this subchapter.

children.heading: Visitor interpretation
children.label: B Visitor interpretation
children.text: The Secretary shall coordinate visitor interpretation of the Nystrom Elementary School site with the West Contra Costa Unified School District.

children.heading: Education center
children.label: c Education center
children.text: The Secretary may establish a World War II Home Front Education Center in the Ford Assembly Building. Such center shall include a program that allows for distance learning and linkages to other representative sites across the country, for the purpose of educating the public as to the significance of the site and the World War II Home Front.

children.label: A
children.text: As a condition of expending any funds appropriated to the Secretary for the purposes of the cooperative agreements under subsection (b)(2), the Secretary shall require that such expenditure must be matched by expenditure of an equal amount of funds, goods, services, or in-kind contributions provided by non-Federal sources.

children.label: B
children.text: With the approval of the Secretary, any donation of property, services, or goods from a non-Federal source may be considered as a contribution of funds from a non-Federal source for purposes of this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: all funds paid by the Secretary to such project; or

children.label: B
children.text: the proportion of the increased value of the project attributable to such payments, determined at the time of such conversion, use, or disposal.

children.heading: Ford Assembly Building
children.label: 1 Ford Assembly Building
children.text: The Secretary may acquire a leasehold interest in the Ford Assembly Building for the purposes of operating a World War II Home Front Education Center.

children.heading: Other facilities
children.label: 2 Other facilities
children.text: The Secretary may acquire, from willing sellers, lands or interests in the Child Development Field Centers (Ruth C. Powers) (Maritime), Atchison Housing, the Kaiser-Permanente Field Hospital, and Richmond Fire Station 67A, through donation, purchase with donated or appropriated funds, transfer from any other Federal agency, or exchange.

children.heading: Artifacts
children.label: 3 Artifacts
children.text: The Secretary may acquire and provide for the curation of historic artifacts that relate to the park.

children.heading: Donations
children.label: f Donations
children.text: The Secretary may accept and use donations of funds, property, and services to carry out this subchapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 3 complete fiscal years after the date funds are made available, the Secretary shall prepare, in consultation with the City of Richmond, California, and transmit to the Committee on Resources of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate a general management plan for the park in accordance with the provisions of and other applicable law.section 100502 of title 54

children.heading: Preservation of setting
children.label: 2 Preservation of setting
children.text: The general management plan shall include a plan to preserve the historic setting of the Rosie the Riveter/World War II Home Front National Historical Park, which shall be jointly developed and approved by the City of Richmond.

children.heading: Additional sites
children.label: 3 Additional sites
children.text: The general management plan shall include a determination of whether there are additional representative sites in Richmond that should be added to the park or sites in the rest of the United States that relate to the industrial, governmental, and citizen efforts during World War II that should be linked to and interpreted at the park. Such determination shall consider any information or findings developed in the National Park Service study of the World War II Home Front under .section 410ggg–2 of this title
